source.article_title: Spatially resolved photoluminescence analysis of the role of Se in CdSe_xTe_1-x thin films

predeclared_primary_field.sample: Cl-treated CdSe_xTe_1-x film fabricated with a 200 nm CdSe underlayer
predeclared_primary_field.selection_reason: The main-text 200 nm CdSe map is a complete registered two-dimensional PL peak-wavelength field with explicit physical coordinates and a source-declared Gaussian peak-fitting operator. It is selected before any numerical-kernel family residual is evaluated.

instrument_contract.scan_mode: snake scan with real-time linear encoder feedback

observation_operator.peak_wavelength_extraction: Gaussian fit to the PL data from each pixel
observation_operator.material_interpretation_from_article: The article interprets longer peak wavelengths at grain boundaries in the 200 nm CdSe sample as evidence of lower local bandgaps, but the R04 demonstration retains the field as a PL peak-wavelength observation and does not relabel it as composition.

allowed: deterministic parsing of the admitted source-data map
variance versus predeclared added numerical kernel scale
comparison with false independent-pixel and independent-scale treatments
predeclared held-out added-kernel scale as a descriptive closure test
phase_2_authorization.required_scale_reporting: pixels, with added-kernel micrometre values permitted only as coordinate conversions; no latent physical correlation length
prohibited: HgCdTe point variance or correlation length
physical deconvolution of the unmeasured native sample-plane kernel
identification of a universal semiconductor covariance family
assignment of PL peak-wavelength variation to alloy composition without an independent observation model
treating numerically smoothed maps as independent physical measurements
treating raster pixels as independent repeats
